skill.name: aws-security-audit
skill.description: Comprehensive AWS security posture assessment using AWS CLI and security best practices
skill.summary: Automated security auditing skill that identifies AWS misconfigurations, IAM risks, and compliance gaps using official AWS CLI tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 74 patterns but all are false positives. The external_commands are legitimate AWS CLI invocations for security auditing. The '0.0.0.0/0' patterns are CIDR queries detecting open security groups, not hardcoded IPs. URLs reference AWS documentation. No malicious intent detected - this is a legitimate security audit tool.

content.value_statement: Security teams struggle to identify AWS misconfigurations and compliance gaps manually. This skill automates comprehensive security assessments using official AWS CLI commands and industry best practices.
actual_capabilities: Scan IAM users for MFA enforcement and unused credentials
Detect overly permissive security groups and public S3 buckets
Identify unencrypted EBS volumes and RDS instances
Verify CloudTrail, Config, and VPC Flow Logs are enabled
Calculate security score with prioritized remediation steps
Map findings to CIS, PCI-DSS, and HIPAA compliance frameworks
limitations: Requires AWS CLI configured with appropriate IAM permissions
Read-only audit tool - does not automatically remediate issues
Cannot assess resources in regions not configured in AWS CLI profile
Some checks may require elevated IAM permissions (SecurityAudit policy or higher)

use_cases.title: Security Engineer Compliance Review
use_cases.description: Run weekly security audits to identify misconfigurations before they become vulnerabilities and maintain compliance evidence for auditors.

use_cases.title: DevOps Pre-Deployment Check
use_cases.description: Validate AWS environment security posture before deploying production applications to ensure no critical gaps exist.

use_cases.title: Cloud Consultant Assessment
use_cases.description: Generate comprehensive security reports for client AWS environments with prioritized remediation recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Security Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the aws-security-audit skill to check my AWS account for basic security issues. Focus on IAM users without MFA and any security groups open to the internet.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute a full AWS security audit using aws-security-audit. Check IAM, network security, data protection, and logging. Calculate my security score and list all issues by priority.

prompt_templates.title: Compliance-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-security-audit to assess my AWS environment against CIS Foundations Benchmark requirements. Highlight any findings that would fail a compliance audit.

prompt_templates.title: Remediation Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Run aws-security-audit and generate a remediation plan. Group findings by critical, high, and medium priority with specific AWS CLI commands to fix each issue.

output_examples.input: Run a comprehensive security audit on my AWS account
output_examples.output: Security Audit Report showing: 3 users without MFA enabled, 2 security groups with unrestricted inbound access, 5 unencrypted EBS volumes, CloudTrail logging active. Security Score: 65/100. Prioritized remediation steps provided.
IAM Security Findings: 2 access keys older than 90 days, 1 inactive user with console access, root account has no MFA. Recommendations include rotating old keys and disabling unused users.
Unencrypted Resources Report: 5 EBS volumes in us-east-1, 1 RDS instance (postgres-prod), 3 S3 buckets without default encryption. Estimated remediation time and CLI commands provided for each.

best_practices: Run security audits weekly and store reports for trend analysis
Automate audits using EventBridge triggers with Lambda for continuous monitoring
Document all exceptions with business justification and compensating controls
anti_patterns: Running audits only once and never reviewing trends over time
Ignoring medium-severity findings that can compound into major risks
Executing audit commands with overly permissive IAM credentials instead of read-only SecurityAudit policy

faq.question: What IAM permissions does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires read-only permissions. AWS recommends attaching the SecurityAudit managed policy which provides the minimum permissions needed for comprehensive security assessments.

faq.question: Does this skill make any changes to my AWS resources?
faq.answer: No. This is a read-only audit skill that only queries your AWS environment. It does not modify, create, or delete any resources. All remediation must be performed manually.

faq.question: How often should I run a security audit?
faq.answer: AWS recommends running comprehensive security audits at least weekly. For high-security environments, consider daily audits with automated alerting on new findings.

faq.question: Can this skill audit multiple AWS accounts?
faq.answer: Yes. Configure AWS CLI profiles for each account and run the audit against each profile. For organizations with many accounts, consider using AWS Security Hub for centralized visibility.

faq.question: What compliance frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The audit maps findings to CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark, PCI-DSS, and HIPAA requirements. Custom mappings can be added for organization-specific compliance needs.

faq.question: How do I fix the issues this skill finds?
faq.answer: The skill provides prioritized remediation guidance with specific AWS CLI commands. Critical issues should be addressed immediately, high-severity within 7 days, and medium within 30 days.
